name: Manhattan

spec.method_detail: Stir with ice ~40 rotations until well-chilled. Strain into chilled coupe. Garnish with expressed orange peel or cherry.

garnish: Expressed orange peel or maraschino cherry
history: The Manhattan is one of the six basic drinks in David Embury's 'Fine Art of Mixing Drinks' and one of the most important cocktails in history. The origin myth involves a banquet at the Manhattan Club for Lady Randolph Churchill (Winston's mother) — this is almost certainly false, as she was abroad at the time. It likely emerged from the cocktail culture of 1880s Manhattan. The rye vs. bourbon debate is regional: rye gives it spice and dryness, bourbon sweetness. Both are correct.
